commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d05b3755a0dfe17fad875c0ba1aa7de0473b10db)
commands.review_summary:
The PR writes `openclaw.schema.json` beside the active config during OpenClaw-owned config writes, stamps missing or blank root `$schema` values, reuses the schema helper for `openclaw config schema`, and updates docs/tests.

PR surface: Source +188, Tests +231, Docs +8. Total +427 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main writes only the config target, while the PR adds mandatory sidecar/root writes before or around the config commit. I did not run a filesystem-permission repro because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review is needed before repair because the narrow code change depends on the product decision for sidecar failure semantics and automatic root stamping.

target.title: feat(config): write local editor schema
target.body: I don’t seem to be able to touch my `openclaw.json` without breaking something, and it’s hard to discover all the available options. This PR adds a small breadcrumb for humans and our clankers: when OpenClaw writes the config, it also leaves behind the local schema that editors need for validation and IntelliSense. ## What changed - Writes `openclaw.schema.json` next to the active `openclaw.json`. - Adds `"$schema": "./openclaw.schema.json"` when the config does not already have a schema. - Keeps existing non-empty `$schema` values alone. - Reuses the same schema helper for `openclaw config schema` and the editor-facing schema file. ## Real behavior proof - Behavior or issue addressed: OpenClaw-owned config writes now create a local editor schema and stamp a local `$schema` reference when one is missing. - Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux, with an isolated temporary `HOME` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` so this did not touch my real `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: ```bash tmp_home=$(mktemp -d) mkdir -p "$tmp_home/.openclaw" config="$tmp_home/.openclaw/openclaw.json" printf '{"gateway":{"mode":"local"}}\n' > "$config" HOME="$tmp_home" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$config" OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 \ node scripts/run-node.mjs config set gateway.port 18790 --strict-json ``` - Evidence after fix: VS Code picks up the local schema and shows IntelliSense. The isolated config write also produced this check output: ```json { "schemaRef": "./openclaw.schema.json", "port": 18790, "schemaFileExists": true, "hasGateway": true, "hasChannels": true, "hasSchemaProperty": { "type": "string" } } ``` - Observed result after fix: `openclaw.json` was updated with the requested config value and a local `$schema` reference, and `openclaw.schema.json` existed beside it with expected root schema fields. - What was not tested: I did not test Nix-managed config because config writers intentionally refuse Nix-mode writes. ## Additional real behavior proof: include-backed JSON5 root This covers the latest ClawSweeper request: an OpenClaw-owned config write against an include-backed JSON5 root where a leading comment contains `{` before the real root object. ```bash $ OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/.openclaw/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 openclaw config set gateway.port 19001 --strict-json Updated gateway.port. Restart the gateway to apply. $ sed -n '1,8p' /.openclaw/openclaw.json // Leading comment with { before the root object. { "$schema": "./openclaw.schema.json", gateway: { $include: "./config/gateway.json5" }, } $ cat /.openclaw/config/gateway.json5 { "port": 19001 } $ node -e 'print schema checks' schema exists: true schema has root $schema property: true schema has gateway property: true ``` Observed result: the leading JSON5 comment was preserved, the comment brace was ignored, `$schema` was inserted into the actual root object, the included `gateway.json5` file received the targeted config change, and `openclaw.schema.json` was written beside the root config. Focused after-fix validation: ```bash node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/mutate.test.ts src/config/io.write-config.test.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check corepack pnpm config:schema:check git diff --check ``` ## Tested ```bash pnpm test src/config/io.write-config.test.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts pnpm tsgo:core:test pnpm config:schema:check pnpm lint:docs git diff --check ``` ## Notes - Nix-managed config is still intentionally immutable; this does not change that path. - Manual edits do not refresh the schema by themselves. The schema is refreshed when OpenClaw owns the config write. - The main review question is whether config IO is the right place to write the adjacent editor schema.
target.author: danhayman

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: danhayman
commands.reason: comment version already processed in ledger
